annotation: Curated 0.4.0 close-out validation matrix. Combines two background-bench runs:
- b3py927cs (clean-state, after LRU cache fix in commit 47162ab): canonical
- b4025zv4y (older run that was thought killed but kept running through ComfyUI
restarts and completed conditions 3, 5, 6; condition 4 timed out under
pre-LRU cache pressure): authoritative wall for condition 3 (only run that
measured it from a clean cache state) and new PSNR comparison for the
Conditions 1+2 (BF16_PROD_SAGE, FP8_PROD_SAGE) ran in an even earlier matrix
before LRU was implemented and are listed here from that run's terminal output.
PSNR pairs use the 0.4.0-rc1 BF16 reference (FP8VAL_BF16_ prefix) where applicable.
Source-of-truth for the README perf table is this file plus the chained CHANGELOG
entries; do not re-run the matrix to UPDATE this file without preserving the
annotation block, since several conditions cannot be cleanly re-measured without
rebuilding the cache state they depended on.
validated_hardware: RTX 5090 (32 GB Blackwell sm_120), Win11, Python 3.12.9, Torch 2.7.0+cu128, ComfyUI Desktop 0.20.1
workflow_baseline: R2AIN_video_api.json @ 480x640x21 frames x 20 steps cfg=5 (unless noted otherwise)

conditions.note: Re-bench PRODUCTION sage baseline (resolve README 14.5 vs B8 10.85)
conditions.source_run: b3u07m45y (pre-LRU; only matrix that measured this condition)
b4025zv4y (clean cold-load on this condition; LRU was working at this point in the run)

conditions.note: Alpha variant FP8 (different code path; uses R2PFB workflow)
conditions.source_run: b3py927cs (clean state after LRU + restart; b4025zv4y's earlier attempt timed out under cache pressure)

conditions.note: PREVIEW under FP8 + sage — chaotic-amplification risk at 4 steps
conditions.source_run: b3py927cs (clean cold-load). b4025zv4y measured the same condition at 11.31 min under cache pressure — not representative.

b4025zv4y_cache_warm.note: Inflated by leftover compile-cache pressure from cond 3.

conditions.note: Text-only mode (no RGB cross-conditioning; uses t2RAIN tiny workflow 256x256x5x3)

b4025zv4y_cache_hit.note: Faster because the FP8 model was already cached from cond 5 in the same run; no cold-load.

FP8_baseline_vs_BF16_baseline._comment: From the 0.4.0-rc1 B8 bench (b8opultjz). Both conditions: 20 steps cfg=5, no sage/compile. The canonical 'how lossy is FP8' measurement.

FP8_PROD_COMPILE_vs_BF16_PROD_SAGE._comment: From b4025zv4y. Compares FP8+compile to BF16+sage. The fact that these PSNR numbers are bit-identical to FP8 vs BF16 baseline is the headline finding: prefer_sage_attn and compile_dit do NOT change output quality; they are purely perf options (and on the validated workload they are perf regressions, see CHANGELOG 0.5.1 follow-up note).

verdict.ship_gate_thresholds: PSNR >= 30 dB albedo / >= 30 dB irradiance / >= 30 dB normal vs BF16 reference
verdict.fastest_config_for_finals: FP8 baseline (dit_weight_mode=fp8_prequantized, no sage, no compile, 20 steps): 9.43 min/chunk
